post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 166, Macbeth Mafia!

If you are not playing in this game, you are not allowed to post in this thread. If you would like to play Mafia, please go sign-up for a game.

Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123/zmann27 for the basic template (many changes have been made).

Story
Go read The Tragedy of Macbeth. It's actually one of Shakespeare's shortest plays, and it's better than anything I could write here.


Read ALL of the rules!
  • Unlike previous games which I have modded, your confirm rule is extremely easy. To confirm, please type the word "Confirm" in bold, so it looks like: Confirm. By doing this, you acknowledge that you may and will be held responsible for anything and everything found under the six large bullet points below.
  • General Rules
    R.1. Read the rules. All of them. Every single one. Including this one. No exceptions. Whatsoever. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    R.2. Bookmark this thread. It makes things easier for everyone. To bookmark, click the button that looks like a bookmark: B. You know that you have done this when the aforementioned button appears like C, E, D or F.
    R.3. Do not post during night, as defined in A.5.
    R.4. Do not post an excessive amount of images or GIF's in your posts. This rule is subject to moderator interpretation. Pre-warnings will be given before I give a warning based on this rule.
    R.5. Do not spam or have excessive white-space in your post. This rule is subject to moderator interpretation. Pre-warnings will be given before I give a warning based on this rule.
    R.6. You may PM me through your Role PM at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification or role clarification. Please do not send me new PM's regarding this game. Note that I cannot reveal anything about other players.
    R.7. Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, with the exception of Role PM's, which are given to you, unless I explicitly give permission. Depending on the severity of the infraction, this may or may not be a modkill level infraction.
    R.8. Roleclaiming and Flavorclaiming are explicitly allowed.
    R.9. Do not talk about this game outside of this thread to anyone other than me (pandabear10), with the exception of any QuickTopics I link to you, unless I explicitly give permission. This is a modkill level infraction. If you are already dead, you may be subject to forum level warnings, based on the Mafia! forum moderators' discretion.
    R.10. You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes, but is not limited to size=1, hide=".", color=#fff, color=transparent, phantom, and color=white. However, breadcrumbing, secret communication in-thread, is allowed, but the breadcrumb must not be hidden in the sense of the breadcrumb's components being invisible. To clarify, a breadcrumb consisting of the first letter in each paragraph is allowed. Using white font is not.
    R.11. Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted under any circumstance. If you would like to clarify or change anything that has already been posted, you may utilize EBWOP (Edit By Way Of Post) or EBWON (Edit By Way Of Post / Edit By Way Of Ninja'd), although anything on the line of "Edit:" will be understood by most players.
    R.12. If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere, including in thread, other than the Dead QuickTopic that you will receive after death, and the Role PM, which is a suitable place to type personal commentary on the game, if you so wish. You may still spectate the game.
    R.13. Play towards your win condition as dictated by the rules and in your Role PM.
    R.14. The only text that I can guarantee that I will read that is posted in-thread is bolded text that is not in a hide tag
    and is at least of font-size 100.     As such, if you want to ask me a question, either use your Role PM, or bold your question.
    R.14. Have fun. If you aren't having fun, this will most likely affect other players. Please just ask for a replacement if you are not having fun.

  • Etiquette
    E.1. Do not use inappropriate language. You kids know what I mean when I say this. If AoPS censors it for you, don't try to circumvent it.
    E.2. Be mature and considerate.
    E.3. Do not make any personal attacks, make sure to attack the person's role. For example, 'I believe you are scum in real life' will draw a warning, but 'You seem scummy' will not draw a warning. Since phrases like, 'I believe you are scum' are often said, a warning in this rule will only be given if it is clearly evident that this rule has been broken intentionally. This rule is subject to moderator interpretation.
    E.4. For minor rule infractions, if a newbie accumulates three warnings, (s)he will be force-replaced. SE's or IC's will be modkilled if two warnings are accumulated.
    E.5. If a player has committed an egregious infraction, then a player will either be force-replaced or modkilled. The moderator and the moderator alone makes the decision to force-replace or modkill, and the decision will be made on a case-by-case basis. However, generally speaking, if game-critical information has not been divulged, a player will be force-replaced. The general exception which will result in a modkill is OOT communication which implicitly or explicitly reveals the role of themselves or others.

  • Activity
    A.1. 'Day' is the time period where players may post in this thread. A mafia-day will normally be about 14 real-life days long, depending on the moderators' availability. Once someone is hanged and the moderator disallows posting for the day, it will become twilight.
    A.2. If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, no hang will automatically take place, and the day will proceed to be twilight.
    A.3. Twilight is the time period between when the moderator tells players not to post and when an end-of-day scene is posted. Once the end-of-day scene is posted, night will immediately commence.
    A.4. A night-action is an action granted by receiving a certain role in-game. The PM will say what their night-action is and how to use it. Night actions may not be submitted during the day. The Vanilla Townie role does not have a night-action.
    A.5. 'Night' is the time period where players who have a designated night-action may submit their night-action. No posts may take place in-thread during this time. Each night will be 3 real-life days long, depending on the moderators' availability. Night may be cut short if all night-actions have been submitted. Once night has been declared over by the moderator, the next day will immediately begin.
    A.6. The moderator may extend the deadline for the day or night at his/her discretion, but this will be unlikely. The two main reasons I might extend the deadline is if someone (including the moderator) is V/LA at the deadline, or if a replacement occurs close to the deadline. However, never count on a deadline extension.
    A.7. When the night deadline is reached, all night-actions must have been submitted. If the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them) before the deadline, the player(s) will lose the chance to perform their action during that night. Mafia may submit their kill either in their role PM or in their QuickTopic. If multiple conflicting kills are submitted, the QuickTopic gets first priority, then the most recent post containing a kill.
    A.8. If a player does not post in a 48-hour period, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
    A.9. If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving an in-game prod as defined in A.8, he/she will receive a PM Prod.
    A.10. If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM Prod as defined in A.9, he/she will be replaced unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
    A.11. If a player accumulates 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced regardless of any future activity. Continued posting will not prevent replacement.
    A.12. If a player is going to be V/LA (Vacation / Leave of Absence), the player should post in bold in-thread, containing the dates of the V/LA. For example, a V/LA declaration can look like, "V/LA from 9/1/2016 - 9/5/2016." Dates will be read by the moderator by default as "mm/dd/yyyy" or "mm/dd/yy". Alternatively, the player may PM me in your designated Role PM thread. I will not give you any prods during a V/LA, but if you request a V/LA longer than 10 days, I will just replace you instead. It is not necessary for formatting to be exact for a V/LA, however, I cannot guarantee that I will catch an in-thread declaration of a V/LA that is not bolded.
    A.13. Players should not ask for semi-V/LA's. Players must use their best judgment to decide if the partial-inactivity is worth declaring a V/LA for. Semi-V/LA's will not be acknowledged under any circumstance.
    A.14. If a player wishes to be replaced, the player must post in bold in-thread something of the form of 'Request Replacement.' I will not be picky with the formatting with this, as long as I can understand that you wish to be replaced. Alternatively, the player may PM me that the party wishes to be replaced.
    A.15. If a replacement for a player cannot be found within 168 hours, and the replaced player does not wish to continue playing or has accumulated five PM prods, then the player will automatically be modkilled.

  • Hangings & Voting
    H.1. Votes are defined as text with the format Vote: <username>, where <username> is a player in the game. Everything in the vote must be properly spaced, spelled, formatted, and capitalized. The only exception to this rule is upper capitalization of the first letter of a username. So, if pandabear10 were playing in this game (he's not, he's your moderator), Vote: Pandabear10 would be acceptable, as well as Vote: pandabear10. Examples of invalid votes include, but are not limited to, Vote pandabear10, vote: pandabear10, Vote: pandabear10, or Vote:pandabear10. Nicknames are also unacceptable, so Vote: panda is also not considered a valid vote. All invalid votes will be ignored.
    H.2. You may vote for no one to be hanged to occur during a day. Vote for this scenario by voting: Vote: No Hang. Rules H.1 - H.2 apply.
    H.3. In the case where a player has the username 'No Hang', you should vote for the player 'No Hang' as you would any other player. To vote for the scenario of no-hang, you should use the format: Vote: No Hang. Rules H.1 - H.2 apply.
    H.4. Unvote exactly in the format "Unvote", with the exception of capitalization (ie: unvote is acceptable). An unvote will remove a current vote if any exists. You do not have to unvote before making a new vote.
    H.5. If at any point in a day other than Day 1 more than half of the players alive are voting for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight, as defined in A.3. Votes are parsed sequentially through a post. If a hang is reached in the middle of a post, any and all subsequent votes will be ignored, even if the person unvotes said vote.
    H.6. If at any point in a day other than Day 1 at least half of the players alive are voting for the scenario of no hang, then the scenario of no hang will occur. It will then immediately be twilight, as defined in A.3.
    H.7. If the current day is Day 1, the day is never terminated early, even if every single player is voting to hang a certain player, or if every single player votes not to hang. The only exception to this rule is if a modkill is deemed necessary, as described in E.6., Once the deadline is reached, a player is hanged according to H.5 and H.6.
    H.7. The moment the deadline passes, or if a hang or no-hang occurs before the deadline after Day 1, no more votes will be counted, even if the mod or co-mod has not made a post confirming a hang.
    H.8. A person that is hanged may still post until the day is officially ended by a moderator.
    H.9. Once I post an end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead, and may no longer post. Night, as defined in A.5, then immediately begins.
    H.10. If a person is killed during night, I will reveal this at the beginning of the next day. The said person or persons will then be officially dead, and may no longer post.

  • Moderator Information
    M.1. I (pandabear10) am the moderator of Mafia Game 166.
    M.2. Blue-colored text (#00f) is used for posts by the moderator (pandabear10). Do not use this color, or anything that looks similar.
    M.3. Red-colored text (#f00) is used for mafia flavor-related posts. However, this color is not reserved, so you may use it.
    M.4. Green-colored text (#0f0) is used for town flavor-related posts. However, this color is not reserved, so you may use it.

  • Setup
    S.1. This game uses the 2d3 setup, a semi-open setup. You are not given the setup, however, you are given a list of nine possible setups. The setup always produces two Mafia players and seven Town players. Setups are choosen randomly.
    S.2. Town's win-condition is for all mafia to be dead.
    S.3. Mafia's win-condition is to reach a situation where one or more mafia cannot die, given that mafia mechanically play optimally, even if a Town player is also unkillable disregarding game-throwing. For example, if the situation is reached where only one Town Doctor and one Mafia Goon are left alive, the Mafia wins, as the Mafia Goon is unkillable, as the Goon can vote to No Hang every day to prevent any hangs even though the Town Doctor can also protect themselves every night to avoid being killed.
    S.4. The nine possible setups are as follows:
    • A1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Neapolitan, 1 Town Cop, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
    • A2. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
    • A3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Cop, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
    • B1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Mafia Rolecop, 1 Mafia Goon
    • B2. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Mafia Rolecop, 1 Mafia Goon
    • B3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Neapolitan, 1 Mafia Rolelp, 1 Mafia Goon
    • C1. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
    • C2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
    • C3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
    S.5. If a Mafia Roleblocker or Mafia Rolecop exists, (s)he may both kill and roleblock/investigate in the same night.
    S.6. If a situation arises in which, in setup A, the Town Jailkeeper jails the Mafia Roleblocker, and the Mafia Roleblocker both roleblocks the Town Jailkeeper and submits the kill, then the Town Jailkeeper will be considered to be roleblocked, while the Mafia Roleblocker will not be considered jailed.
    S.7. Cops and Neapolitans are always sane - they will always either receive the truth or will not get a result entirely. (The latter will only occur if the Cop or Neapolitan is roleblocked in some manner.)
    S.8. The Jailkeeper and Mafia Roleblocker may not jail or roleblock themselves for paradoxical reasons.
    S.9. Mafia may use their designated QuickTopic at all times, including the day. However, all private communication pertaining to this game must be in the QuickTopic. In other words, a private PM, even with me included, is not permissible.
    S.10. Mafia are not allowed to Night-Kill themselves, as this would violate R.13.

Role PM's
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a Regular Person, aka Vanilla Townie. You know about Macbeth's treacherous and evil reign, but by yourself, you have no power to stop him. You must discover true loyalties for Macbeth's end while avoiding his henchmen. You have no night actions. Your power comes from your freedom of speech and your vote. You win when all mafia members are dead.
Town Jailkeeper wrote:
You are the Porter, aka Town Jailkeeper. You control the gates like a mad-man. No surprise you can control what people do. Each night, PM me the name of one person you would like to block (excluding yourself). That person will be protected from night kills, but unable to perform their nightly action, unless you are roleblocked. If you roleblock the Mafia Roleblocker and he/she roleblocks you, their action takes precedence over yours. You win when all mafia members are dead.
Town Neapolitan wrote:
You are Ross, aka Town Neapolitan. When you think, you realize that something is wrong with this situation; something is wrong with the world. However, your conclusions are not as definitive as you would like them to be. Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to attempt to investigate. You will receive a result of Vanilla Townie, Not Vanilla Townie, or that you failed to inspect (you were blocked somehow). You win when all mafia members are dead.
Town Cop wrote:
You are Malcolm, aka Town Cop. You have masterful skills in inference and lying, allowing you to deduce people's alliances. Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to attempt to investigate. You will receive a result of Town, Mafia, or that you failed to inspect (you were blocked somehow). You win when all mafia members are dead.
Town Doctor wrote:
You are a Doctor, aka Town Doctor, obviously. With your *modern* medicinal technology, you are able to prevent someone from dying each night. Each night you may PM me the name of one player (including yourself) to protect. That night, any kills that target that player will fail (unless you are roleblocked). You win when all mafia members are dead.
Town Tracker wrote:
You are Macduff, aka Town Tracker. You, having tracked down Malcolm, now are trying to track down any traitors sneaking about. Each night you may name one player to track. You will receive a result at the beginning of the next day telling you who the player the person targeted for a night action (if any), or that the track failed (you were blocked somehow). You win when all mafia members are dead.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a Murderer, aka Mafia Goon. You have been hired and convinced by Macbeth that his reign is, at least, better than what it would be otherwise. You may talk with your partner, <username>, Mafia Goon / Roleblocker / Rolecop in this quicktopic at any time. Each night, you or your partner may attempt to kill one person. Unless something interferes, the following day the kill will be announced. You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are Macbeth, aka Mafia Roleblocker. You've murdered Duncan and Banquo, and now you are king of Scotland. But your reign is in great peril - your great reign is in danger of toppling. You may talk with your partner, <username>, Mafia Goon, in this quicktopic at any time. Each night, you or your partner may attempt to kill one person. Unless something interferes, the following day the kill will be announced. I would appreciate it if kills are submitted both in the PM and in the quicktopic.
In addition, <flavor>. Each night you may PM me the username of a person to roleblock (excluding yourself). That person's night action will become ineffective that night. If you roleblock the Jailkeeper and he roleblocks you, your roleblock will have precedence over theirs. You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation.
Mafia Rolecop wrote:
You are Lady Macbeth, aka Mafia Rolecop. You are sleepless. Great. However, the fact that you are sleepless allows you to determine who other people are during the night, peering through their disguises. During the night, you may talk with your partner, <username>, Mafia Goon, in this quicktopic. Each night, you or your partner may attempt to kill one person. Unless something interferes, the following day the kill will be announced. I would appreciate it if kills are submitted both in the PM and in the quicktopic.
In addition, <flavor>. Each night you may PM me the username of a person to investigate. At the start of the next day, you will receive the role of the requested player (unless you are roleblocked). You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation.


Alive (6)
The number next to the corresponding name represents the amount of PM Prods the player has received.
  • ++ e_is_cool (1)
  • tikkitamatoa chess24
  • fishy15 (1)
  • + atomicpianowitch kevinmathz
  • hdgehrke
  • relay400 yamyamx2

Significant Events
SFGiantsFan, Regular Person (Vanilla Townie), was hanged Day 1.

jeffisepic, Regular Person (Vanilla Townie), was killed Night 1.

kevinmathz replaces atomicpianowitch.


Warnings
pandabear10, Moderator, has been modkilled for impersonating the moderator.


You may post.

Deadline (Day 2) is on July 3, 2018, 9:00 AM PST.

Have fun!

[b]A.12.[/b] If a player is going to be V/LA (Vacation / Leave of Absence), the player should post in [b]bold[/b] in-thread, containing the dates of the V/LA. For example, a V/LA declaration can look like, "[b]V/LA from 9/1/2016 - 9/5/2016.[/b]" Dates will be read by the moderator by default as "[u]mm/dd/yyyy[/u]" or "[u]mm/dd/yy[/u]". Alternatively, the player may PM me in your designated Role PM thread. I will not give you any prods during a V/LA, but if you request a V/LA longer than 10 days, I will just replace you instead. It is not necessary for formatting to be exact for a V/LA, however, I cannot guarantee that I will catch an in-thread declaration of a V/LA that is not bolded.
[b]A.13.[/b] [b]Players should not ask for semi-V/LA's.[/b] Players must use their best judgment to decide if the partial-inactivity is worth declaring a V/LA for. Semi-V/LA's will not be acknowledged under any circumstance. 
[b]A.14.[/b] If a player wishes to be replaced, the player must post in [b]bold[/b] in-thread something of the form of '[b]Request Replacement.[/b]' I will not be picky with the formatting with this, as long as I can understand that you wish to be replaced. Alternatively, the player may PM me that the party wishes to be replaced.
[b]A.15.[/b] If a replacement for a player cannot be found within 168 hours, and the replaced player does not wish to continue playing or has accumulated five PM prods, then the player will automatically be modkilled. 
[/hide]

[*] [hide="Hangings & Voting"]
[b]H.1.[/b] Votes are defined as text with the format [b]Vote: <username>[/b], where <username> is a player in the game. [u]Everything in the vote must be properly spaced, spelled, formatted, and capitalized.[/u] The [b]only[/b] exception to this rule is upper capitalization of the first letter of a username. So, if pandabear10 were playing in this game (he's not, he's your moderator), [b]Vote: Pandabear10[/b] would be acceptable, as well as [b]Vote: pandabear10[/b]. Examples of invalid votes include, but are not limited to, [b]Vote pandabear10[/b], [b]vote: pandabear10[/b], [i]Vote: pandabear10[/i], or [b]Vote:pandabear10[/b]. Nicknames are also unacceptable, so [b]Vote: panda[/b] is also not considered a valid vote. All invalid votes will be ignored. 
[b]H.2.[/b] You may vote for no one to be hanged to occur during a day. Vote for this scenario by voting: [b]Vote: No Hang[/b]. Rules [b]H.1 - H.2[/b] apply.
[b]H.3.[/b] In the case where a player has the username 'No Hang', you should vote for the [i]player[/i] 'No Hang' as you would any other player. To vote for the [i]scenario[/i] of no-hang, you should use the format: [b]Vote: [u]No Hang[/u][/b]. Rules [b]H.1 - H.2[/b] apply.
[b]H.4.[/b] Unvote exactly in the format "[b]Unvote[/b]", with the exception of capitalization (ie: [b]unvote[/b] is acceptable). An unvote will remove a current vote if any exists. You do not have to unvote before making a new vote. 
[b]H.5.[/b] If at any point in a day other than Day 1 [b]more[/b] than half of the players alive are voting for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight, as defined in [b]A.3[/b]. Votes are parsed sequentially through a post. If a hang is reached in the middle of a post, any and all subsequent votes will be ignored, even if the person unvotes said vote.
[b]H.6.[/b] If at any point in a day other than Day 1 [b]at least[/b] half of the players alive are voting for the scenario of no hang, then the scenario of no hang will occur. It will then immediately be twilight, as defined in [b]A.3[/b].
[b]H.7.[/b] [b]If the current day is Day 1[/b], the day is never terminated early, even if every single player is voting to hang a certain player, or if every single player votes not to hang. The only exception to this rule is if a modkill is deemed necessary, as described in [b]E.6.[/b], Once the deadline is reached, a player is hanged according to [b]H.5[b] and [b]H.6.[/b]
[b]H.7.[/b] The moment the deadline passes, or if a hang or no-hang occurs before the deadline after Day 1, no more votes will be counted, even if the mod or co-mod has not made a post confirming a hang.
[b]H.8.[/b] A person that is hanged may still post until the day is officially ended by a moderator.
[b]H.9.[/b] Once I post an end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead, and may no longer post. Night, as defined in [b]A.5[/b], then immediately begins.
[b]H.10.[/b] If a person is killed during night, I will reveal this at the beginning of the next day. The said person or persons will then be officially dead, and may no longer post.
[/hide]

[*] [hide="Moderator Information"]
[b]M.1.[/b] I (pandabear10) am the moderator of Mafia Game 166.
[b]M.2.[/b] Blue-colored text [color=#00f](#00f)[/color] is used for posts by the moderator (pandabear10).[b] Do not use this color, or anything that looks similar.[/b]
[b]M.3.[/b] Red-colored text [color=#f00](#f00)[/color] is used for mafia flavor-related posts. However, this color is not reserved, so you may use it.
[b]M.4.[/b] Green-colored text [color=#0f0](#0f0)[/color] is used for town flavor-related posts. However, this color is not reserved, so you may use it.
[/hide]

[*][hide="Setup"]
[b]S.1.[/b] This game uses the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=2d3]2d3[/url] setup, a [b]semi-open setup[/b]. You are not given the setup, however, you are given a list of nine possible setups. The setup always produces two [color=#f00]Mafia[/color] players and seven [color=#0f0]Town[/color] players. Setups are choosen randomly.
[b]S.2.[/b] [color=#0f0]Town's[/color] win-condition is for all mafia to be dead. 
[b]S.3.[/b] [color=#f00]Mafia's[/color] win-condition is to reach a situation where one or more mafia cannot die, given that mafia mechanically play optimally, even if a Town player is also unkillable disregarding game-throwing. For example, if the situation is reached where only one Town Doctor and one Mafia Goon are left alive, the Mafia wins, as the Mafia Goon is unkillable, as the Goon can vote to No Hang every day to prevent any hangs even though the Town Doctor can also protect themselves every night to avoid being killed.
[b]S.4.[/b] The nine possible setups are as follows: [list]
[*] A1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Neapolitan, 1 Town Cop, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] A2. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] A3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Cop, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] B1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Mafia Rolecop, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] B2. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Mafia Rolecop, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] B3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Neapolitan, 1 Mafia Rolelp, 1 Mafia Goon
[*] C1. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] C2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] C3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
[/list]
[b]S.5.[/b] If a Mafia Roleblocker or Mafia Rolecop exists, (s)he may both kill and roleblock/investigate in the same night.
[b]S.6.[/b] If a situation arises in which, in setup A, the Town Jailkeeper jails the Mafia Roleblocker, and the Mafia Roleblocker both roleblocks the Town Jailkeeper and submits the kill, then the Town Jailkeeper will be considered to be roleblocked, while the Mafia Roleblocker will [b]not[/b] be considered jailed.
[b]S.7.[/b] Cops and Neapolitans are always sane - they will always either receive the truth or will not get a result entirely. (The latter will only occur if the Cop or Neapolitan is roleblocked in some manner.)
[b]S.8.[/b] The Jailkeeper and Mafia Roleblocker may not jail or roleblock themselves for paradoxical reasons.
[b]S.9.[/b] Mafia may use their designated QuickTopic [b]at all times, including the day.[/b] However, all private communication pertaining to this game must be in the QuickTopic. In other words, a private PM, even with me included, is [b]not[/b] permissible.
[b]S.10.[/b] Mafia are not allowed to Night-Kill themselves, as this would violate [b]R.13[/b].
[/hide]
[/list]

[hide="Role PM's"][quote=Vanilla Townie]You are a Regular Person, aka [color=#0f0]Vanilla Townie[/color]. You know about Macbeth's treacherous and evil reign, but by yourself, you have no power to stop him. You must discover true loyalties for Macbeth's end while avoiding his henchmen. You have no night actions. Your power comes from your freedom of speech and your vote. You win when all mafia members are dead.[/quote]

[quote=Town Jailkeeper]You are the Porter, aka [color=#0f0]Town Jailkeeper[/color]. You control the gates like a mad-man. No surprise you can control what people do. Each night, PM me the name of one person you would like to block (excluding yourself). That person will be protected from night kills, but unable to perform their nightly action, unless you are roleblocked. If you roleblock the Mafia Roleblocker and he/she roleblocks you, their action takes precedence over yours. You win when all mafia members are dead.[/quote]

[quote=Town Neapolitan]You are Ross, aka [color=#0f0]Town Neapolitan[/color]. When you think, you realize that something is wrong with this situation; something is wrong with the world. However, your conclusions are not as definitive as you would like them to be. Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to attempt to investigate. You will receive a result of Vanilla Townie, Not Vanilla Townie, or that you failed to inspect (you were blocked somehow). You win when all mafia members are dead.[/quote]

[quote=Town Cop]You are Malcolm, aka [color=#0f0]Town Cop[/color]. You have masterful skills in inference and lying, allowing you to deduce people's alliances. Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to attempt to investigate. You will receive a result of Town, Mafia, or that you failed to inspect (you were blocked somehow). You win when all mafia members are dead.[/quote]

[quote=Town Doctor]You are a Doctor, aka [color=#0f0]Town Doctor[/color], obviously. With your *modern* medicinal technology, you are able to prevent someone from dying each night. Each night you may PM me the name of one player (including yourself) to protect. That night, any kills that target that player will fail (unless you are roleblocked). You win when all mafia members are dead.[/quote]

[quote=Town Tracker]You are Macduff, aka [color=#0f0]Town Tracker[/color]. You, having tracked down Malcolm, now are trying to track down any traitors sneaking about. Each night you may name one player to track. You will receive a result at the beginning of the next day telling you who the player the person targeted for a night action (if any), or that the track failed (you were blocked somehow). You win when all mafia members are dead.[/quote]

[quote=Mafia Goon]You are a Murderer, aka [color=#f00]Mafia Goon[/color]. You have been hired and convinced by Macbeth that his reign is, at least, better than what it would be otherwise. You may talk with your partner, <username>, Mafia Goon / Roleblocker / Rolecop in this [url=www.example.com]quicktopic[/url] at any time. Each night, you or your partner may attempt to kill one person. Unless something interferes, the following day the kill will be announced. You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation.[/quote]

[quote=Mafia Roleblocker]You are Macbeth, aka [color=#f00]Mafia Roleblocker[/color]. You've murdered Duncan and Banquo, and now you are king of Scotland. But your reign is in great peril - your great reign is in danger of toppling. You may talk with your partner, <username>, [color=#f00]Mafia Goon[/color], in this [url=www.example.com]quicktopic[/url] at any time. Each night, you or your partner may attempt to kill one person. Unless something interferes, the following day the kill will be announced. I would appreciate it if kills are submitted both in the PM and in the quicktopic. 
In addition, <flavor>. Each night you may PM me the username of a person to roleblock (excluding yourself). That person's night action will become ineffective that night. If you roleblock the Jailkeeper and he roleblocks you, your roleblock will have precedence over theirs. You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation.[/quote]

[quote=Mafia Rolecop]You are Lady Macbeth, aka [color=#f00]Mafia Rolecop[/color]. You are sleepless. Great. However, the fact that you are sleepless allows you to determine who other people are during the night, peering through their disguises. During the night, you may talk with your partner, <username>, [color=#f00]Mafia Goon[/color], in this [url=www.example.com]quicktopic[/url]. Each night, you or your partner may attempt to kill one person. Unless something interferes, the following day the kill will be announced. I would appreciate it if kills are submitted both in the PM and in the quicktopic. 
In addition, <flavor>. Each night you may PM me the username of a person to investigate. At the start of the next day, you will receive the role of the requested player (unless you are roleblocked). You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation.[/quote][/hide]

But hedging can be scummy when the person A reading person B has evidence for person B being scum, but knows that they really should be townreading person B, so they say "but they're also towny" or they just have a really lopsided argument and end up in the middle because of conflicted motives and not knowing where to stand. 

Also, reading through the entire game rn, I'll have a full reads list when I'm done.

post_rendered:
Many people asked me why I did not end the game at the yamyamx2 hang. It is definitely true that Mafia have a definite win given the setup (A1). However, I did not end day for the following reasons:

1. The big one for me is consistency. I want to execute modding exactly the same way if there was a Town Doctor in this scenario as opposed to a Town Cop. Executing differently may result in encouraging 'reading the mod' or using the behavior of the mod to determine x, y, or z. Hence, since a Vanilla townie could not have known the setup (definitively; I am aware that kevinmathz did roleclaim), as only BZ1, the Neapolitan, had been mod-flipped, I refrained from ending the game early to avoid issues with consistency.

2. Technically, Mafia hadn't won yet. If, for some reason, Mafia went inactive and failed to submit a kill, D3 would occur with three townies alive. I am aware that this would be a pretty big throw on the Mafia's part but it is a possibility. I am aware that my win-condition for Mafia reads "You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation," of which it is true that failing to submit a kill is 'cooperation.' I will change the wording of this rule in the future, and the reason why I decided to override this is mainly because of point 1.

3. I think the main thing going on in my head (which proved to not be true apparently) is that people wouldn't mind extending the game for three more days to really wrap the game up. By the PM's I received about this, this clearly isn't true. Sorry.
post_canonical:

1. The big one for me is consistency. I want to execute modding exactly the same way if there was a Town Doctor in this scenario as opposed to a Town Cop. Executing differently may result in encouraging 'reading the mod' or using the behavior of the mod to determine x, y, or z. Hence, since a Vanilla townie could not have known the setup (definitively; I am aware that kevinmathz did roleclaim), as only BZ1, the Neapolitan, had been mod-flipped, I refrained from ending the game early to avoid issues with consistency.

2. Technically, Mafia hadn't won yet. If, for some reason, Mafia went inactive and failed to submit a kill, D3 would occur with three townies alive. I am aware that this would be a pretty big throw on the Mafia's part but it is a possibility. I am aware that my win-condition for Mafia reads "You win when it is impossible for town to arrest all mafia without your cooperation," of which it is true that failing to submit a kill is 'cooperation.' I will change the wording of this rule in the future, and the reason why I decided to override this is mainly because of point 1.

3. I think the main thing going on in my head (which proved to not be true apparently) is that people wouldn't mind extending the game for three more days to [i]really[/i] wrap the game up. By the PM's I received about this, this clearly isn't true. Sorry. :([/color]
